title: Structural Fire (Building)
steps: Get out, stay out, and call emergency services.
If you see smoke, stay low to the floor where the air is cleaner.
Before opening any door, touch the door handle or door with the back of your hand; if it is hot, do NOT open it.
If your clothes catch fire: Stop, Drop, and Roll.
Do NOT use elevators during a fire.
If you are trapped, close all doors between you and the fire, and use cloth to seal cracks around doors.

title: Electrical Fire
steps: Do NOT attempt to use water on an electrical fire (risk of electrocution).
Identify the source and, if safe, turn off the main power supply.
Use a Class C (or CO2/Dry Powder) fire extinguisher if available.
Do not touch any wires or appliances involved in the fire.
If the fire is inside a wall or appliance, evacuate immediately and call emergency services.
Do not turn the power back on until a certified electrician has inspected the damage.

title: Wildfire / Forest Fire
steps: Monitor local news and emergency alerts for evacuation orders.
If ordered to evacuate, leave immediately. Do NOT wait.
Close all windows and doors to prevent embers from entering the structure.
If trapped outdoors, find an area clear of vegetation (like a rocky area or a body of water).
Stay low to the ground and cover your mouth with a damp cloth to filter smoke.
Wear long-sleeved clothing made of natural fibers (cotton/wool) to protect against heat.

title: Chimney Fire
steps: Evacuate everyone from the house immediately.
Call emergency services.
If safe, close the fireplace doors or dampen the air intake to starve the fire of oxygen.
Do NOT throw water down the chimney (can cause explosive steam or crack the flue).
Wait outside for the fire department to arrive.
Do not use the fireplace again until inspected and cleaned by a professional.

title: Wildfire Survival in Vehicle
steps: If you cannot escape, park the car away from heavy vegetation.
Turn on the headlights and hazard lights to stay visible.
Close all windows and vents. Turn the AC to 'recirculate'.
Get on the floor of the car and cover yourself with a wool or cotton blanket/coat.
Stay in the car until the main fire front passes; the car's body provides protection against radiant heat.
Expect the car to shake and fill with light smoke. Stay low and breathe through a damp cloth.

title: Kitchen Grease Fire
steps: Do NOT throw water on a grease fire (it will cause an explosion of flames).
If safe, turn off the heat source immediately.
Smother the flames by sliding a metal lid or cookie sheet over the pan.
Leave the pan covered until it has cooled completely.
Do NOT attempt to move the pan or carry it outside.
If the fire spreads beyond the pan, evacuate and call emergency services.

title: Clothing Fire (Stop, Drop, Roll)
steps: STOP immediately. Do not run, as running fans the flames and makes the fire grow.
DROP to the ground and cover your face with your hands.
ROLL back and forth until the fire is out.
If someone else is on fire, smother the flames with a heavy blanket or coat.
Once the fire is out, cool the burned area with cool (not cold) water for 10-20 minutes.
Do not apply ice, ointments, or bandages to a severe burn; seek medical help.

title: Office / Workplace Fire
steps: Activate the nearest fire alarm pull station immediately.
Evacuate using the nearest safe exit or stairs. Never use elevators.
If you are the last one out, close doors behind you to contain the fire.
Proceed to the designated assembly area outside.
If trapped, stay in a room with a window and call 112/911. Signal your location through the window.
Account for all colleagues and report missing persons to the fire department.

title: Boat / Vessel Fire
steps: Alert everyone on board and ensure all passengers have life jackets on.
Stop the boat or position it so the wind blows the fire away from the vessel.
If the fire is in the engine space, shut off fuel valves and use the fire port to discharge an extinguisher.
Radio for help (Mayday or Pan-Pan) with your exact coordinates.
Prepare to abandon ship if the fire cannot be controlled.
If you must enter the water, stay together and stay away from any burning fuel on the surface.

title: Vehicle Battery Fire (EV / Hybrid)
steps: Immediately pull over and evacuate all passengers to a safe distance (at least 50 meters).
Call emergency services and specify that an Electric Vehicle (EV) is on fire.
BEWARE: EV fires can release highly toxic gases (fluorides) and can experience 'thermal runaway'.
Do NOT attempt to extinguish a large battery fire with a standard fire extinguisher.
Expect the fire to be extremely intense and difficult to put out; it may bridge/re-ignite hours later.
Stay upwind to avoid inhalation of toxic smoke.

title: High-Rise Building Fire
steps: Check the stairwell for smoke before entering; if it is smoke-filled, return to your room.
Never use elevators. They can become traps and may open on the fire floor.
If you cannot evacuate, stay in your room, seal the door with wet towels, and call emergency services.
Signal your location by hanging a brightly colored cloth or using a flashlight at the window.
If there is smoke in the room, stay low to the floor where the air is fresher.
Do NOT break windows unless absolutely necessary (broken glass can injure people below and provide oxygen to the fire).

title: Gas Station / Fuel Fire
steps: If a fire starts while you are fueling, do NOT remove the nozzle from the car (this will spray fire).
Immediately hit the emergency shut-off button at the pump or station office.
Evacuate everyone from the vehicle and the immediate area of the fire.
Call emergency services and warn others to stay away.
Do NOT start your engine or use any electronic devices near the fuel spill or fire.
Wait at a safe distance (at least 100 meters) for the fire department to arrive.
